problem_summary: a Thai Noodle Bar operates a technically excellent website with PageSpeed scores of 97 on mobile and 99 on desktop, a functional booking system, and a strong Google review reputation of 4.6 across 102 reviews. Despite this strong foundation, the business's reputation is not currently confirmed to be visible on the website itself at the point where first-time visitors decide to book. A targeted set of additions could make existing trust signals work harder without changing anything that is already performing well.
recommended_solution: A focused, fixed-scope conversion sprint delivering on-site review surfacing, local schema implementation, and a lightweight content section — completed within two weeks with no disruption to current site performance.
scope_of_work: Embed two to three curated Google review excerpts with star ratings on the homepage and in proximity to the booking CTA, ensuring trust signals are visible at the moment visitors decide to reserve a table.

Implement and verify local Restaurant schema markup covering address, opening hours, cuisine type, and phone number to strengthen the site's eligibility for location-based search results relevant to Westport dining queries.

Set up a lightweight content section for seasonal menus or short updates, with a simple self-service template so the business can manage updates independently or hand off to a support arrangement going forward.
expected_outcomes: Surfacing curated reviews near the booking CTA should improve booking confidence for first-time visitors who have not previously encountered the restaurant through word of mouth.

Verified local schema markup is likely to improve the site's visibility for location-based search queries, supporting organic discovery from tourists and visitors searching for dining options in Westport.
A structured content section reduces the need for ad hoc site edits and creates a repeatable format for adding locally relevant, indexable content during peak tourist seasons.
next_steps: Schedule a 15-minute call to confirm which elements of the scope are already in place and agree on a start date that suits the business ahead of the busy season.
risks_if_delayed: Westport's seasonal visitor traffic represents a time-sensitive opportunity, and each week without on-site reviews or local schema in place is a period during which first-time visitors and tourists searching for dining options may choose an alternative that appears more credible or visible in local search results.
